scores.correctness_notes: All YAML fields are accurate: services with override/command/startup/environment/on-check-failure, checks with http/level/period/timeout/threshold, log-targets with type/location/services/labels. Extra fields like user, group, working-dir, after, and a second readiness-check are all valid Pebble features. Minor omission: explanation says only loki/syslog are supported, missing opentelemetry, but this is a documentation gap not a factual error in the YAML itself.
scores.hallucination_notes: none — all fields (after, user, group, working-dir, services list in log-targets, X-Probe header) are real Pebble layer spec features. The charm.py example uses real ops APIs (add_layer, replan, get_plan).
